name: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA)
summary: The Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act requires financial institutions to explain their information-sharing practices to customers and to safeguard sensitive data. Its Financial Privacy Rule, Safeguards Rule, and Pretexting provisions establish a framework for protecting nonpublic personal information (NPI) held by financial institutions including banks, securities firms, and insurance companies.
keyRequirements: Financial Privacy Rule: clear privacy notices to customers describing data collection and sharing practices
Opt-out rights for consumers before sharing NPI with nonaffiliated third parties
Safeguards Rule: comprehensive information security program with administrative, technical, and physical safeguards
Designated qualified individual to oversee and implement the information security program
Regular risk assessments and testing of security controls
Pretexting provisions: prohibit obtaining customer information through false pretenses
Service provider oversight and contractual security requirements
penalties: Financial institutions face fines up to $100,000 per violation. Officers and directors face personal fines up to $10,000 per violation. Imprisonment up to 5 years for individuals. FTC, banking regulators, and state attorneys general enforce compliance.
